name: Cerro El Viso — circular desde Zulema
region_intro: Hiking trails around Alcalá De Henares are primarily found within the Parque Natural Los Cerros, an expansive natural area along the Henares River. The region features diverse landscapes including riverine environments, valleys, and pine forests. Prominent hills like Ecce Homo and Cerro El Viso offer elevated viewpoints across the surrounding terrain. Historical sites such as the Alcalá la Vieja fortress are integrated into several routes, providing a blend of natural and cultural exploration. Best hiking trails around Alcalá De Henares The most popular hiking route is Alcalá La Vieja y cima del Ecce-Homo – circular por el Parque Natural Los Cerros, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends to the Ecce Homo hill and visits the ruins of Alcalá la Vieja. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to Ecce Homo — round trip from Alcalá de Henares, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views from the highest point in Alcalá. Local hikers also love the Ruta de la Puerta Verde — Parque de los Cerros de Alcalá circular, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through the Parque de los Cerros, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Hiking around Alcalá De Henares is defined by riverine areas along the Henares, rolling hills, and pine forests, often incorporating historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Alcalá De Henares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcalá De Henares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Alcalá De Henares?
A: Alcalá De Henares offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 100 routes to explore. These trails cater to different preferences, including easy walks, moderate hikes, and a few more challenging options.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Alcalá De Henares?
A: Hiking in Alcalá De Henares primarily takes you through the expansive Parque Natural Los Cerros. Here, you'll encounter diverse landscapes, including riverine areas along the Henares River, picturesque valleys, and peaceful pine forests. Prominent hills like Ecce Homo and Cerro El Viso offer elevated viewpoints with spectacular panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore on the trails?
A: Yes, several routes integrate historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957340174/alcala-la-vieja-y-cima-del-ecce-homo-circular-por-el-parque-natural-los-cerros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá La Vieja y cima del Ecce-Homo – circular por el Parque Natural Los Cerros&lt;/a&gt; route visits the ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2393087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá la Vieja Fortress&lt;/a&gt;, a 9th-century Arab castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318415473/arab-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arab Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; also leads to these significant ruins, offering a blend of nature and history.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Alcalá De Henares?
A: Yes, many trails in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306661633/ruta-de-la-puerta-verde-parque-de-los-cerros-de-alcala-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Puerta Verde — Parque de los Cerros de Alcalá circular&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the heart of the Parque de los Cerros.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Alcalá De Henares?
A: The hiking routes in Alcalá De Henares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, the blend of natural beauty with historical elements, and the panoramic views from elevated points like Ecce Homo.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Alcalá De Henares?
A: Yes, the Parque Natural Los Cerros offers several easy and accessible trails suitable for families, including those with young children. The 'Ruta de los Tarayes' is specifically mentioned as an easy and simple route, perfect for experiencing the park's natural and human-influenced environments.

Q: Are there trails with good viewpoints in Alcalá De Henares?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298672464/ascent-to-ecce-homo-round-trip-from-alcala-de-henares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Ecce Homo — round trip from Alcalá de Henares&lt;/a&gt; leads to the highest point, offering spectacular panoramic views of Madrid and Guadalajara. Another great option for extensive views is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306766051/cerro-el-viso-circular-desde-zulema" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro El Viso — circular desde Zulema&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: How can I get to the hiking trails using public transport?
A: Alcalá de Henares is well-connected by public transport, making it accessible for hikers. Many trails, especially those within the Parque Natural Los Cerros, are reachable from the town center, which has bus and train connections. Specific public transport options to trailheads can vary, so it's advisable to check local bus routes to the park entrances.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Alcalá De Henares?
A: Yes, there are usually parking facilities available near the main access points to the Parque Natural Los Cerros, which is where most of the hiking trails are located. While specific parking areas can vary, the park is designed to be accessible for visitors arriving by car.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcalá De Henares?
A: The region offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for hiking. However, with proper preparation, trails can be enjoyed in winter, and early mornings or late afternoons are ideal for summer hikes to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique natural features to see?
A: The Parque Natural Los Cerros itself is a significant natural area, featuring diverse environments from riverbeds along the Henares to pine and tamarix woods. You might also encounter natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3031028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Grande de Corpa&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1737135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Oliva fountain&lt;/a&gt;, which add to the natural charm of the area.
tips: Cerro El Viso or San Juan del Viso or Zulema offers us views of the southern part of Torreón de Ardoz and adjacent to Alcalá de Henares.
Views of the town of Torres de Alameda and its green fields. The routes of Cerro El Viso are worth traveling at any time of the day, the landscapes are beautiful.
From this point we have wide views of Villalbilla, Los Hueros and Torres de la Alameda (described from left to right).
